title: foundation — HTTP middleware
description: Four base middleware every downstream module composes on top of. Registered by `FoundationServiceProvider::boot()` via `Router::aliasMiddleware()` + assembled into the global `api` middleware group. API + payload version resolution (previously `api.version` here) has moved to `modules/versioning/` — that module owns the ApiVersion entity + the resolver chain + the RFC 8594 Sunset header emitter. The priority order at the bottom is the canonical stack including versioning's slot.

middleware.description: Reads inbound `X-Request-ID` header or generates a fresh UUIDv7 if absent. Binds the value into the request-scoped container, the log context (`Log::withContext(['request_id' => $id])`), and emits it back on the response header. Cornerstone of distributed tracing — every downstream service reads this header, every log line carries it, every event dispatched during the request includes it as metadata.
sideEffects: Attaches to Sentry scope as `request_id` tag

middleware.description: Wraps controller responses into the canonical `{ data, meta, links }` shape (or `{ message, error, errors }` for error responses). Idempotent — if the response is already enveloped, passes through unchanged. Handles: successful collection responses (adds `meta.pagination` from LengthAwarePaginator), single-resource responses (bare data), errors (wraps ApiException into the error envelope), and streamed responses (skips envelope entirely).

Global rate limit applied on top of every route. Framework's ThrottleRequests with a foundation-configured RateLimiter binding. Base limit: 60 requests / minute per authenticated user OR per IP (unauthenticated). Every module's per-feature limiter composes ADDITIVELY on top — a 429 from throttle.invitations does not consume the base counter.
sideEffects: Emits X-RateLimit-Limit + X-RateLimit-Remaining on every response

middleware.description: CORS enforcement with a per-Application origin allow-list. Reads `application.cors_origins` from the resolved Application (via tenancy's ResolveTenant). Refuses requests from origins not on the allow-list with 403. Allow-list is per-Application because the EU deployment has different customer origins than the US deployment.
sideEffects: Emits Access-Control-Allow-Origin (echoing the request Origin when allow-listed)
Emits Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true (Sanctum cookie sessions require)

middleware.description: Specialised throttle for the health surface. 300 req/min per IP for /api/ping + /up; 60 req/min for /api/health.

api.version.reason: API + payload + webhook payload versioning are all instances of the same lifecycle concern. Versioning module owns the ApiVersion entity + resolver chain (URL + header + query + webhook + GraphQL) + deprecation workflow + payload transformer registry.

notes: response.envelope has priority 200 to guarantee it runs LAST — any earlier response wrapper would double-wrap.
request.id has priority 5 to guarantee it runs FIRST — every subsequent middleware needs the request_id in the log context.
throttle.base composes ADDITIVELY with per-feature limiters. A 429 from a feature limiter does not consume the base counter.
versioning.resolve slots in at priority 30 (was foundation's api.version priority) — declared by modules/versioning/middleware.json but placed in this canonical order.
